consit.about: Established in 1951, this lower Cauvery delta constituency is a major agricultural hub home to the Mahalingaswamy Temple. It is historically significant for its brass-vessel cottage industries and remains a key center for the agrarian labor class.

problem.title: Handloom Wage Arrears
problem.desc: Weavers in Thirubhuvanam have not received wages from cooperative societies for over 4 months, leading to widespread financial distress.

problem.title: Tail-end Water Scarcity
problem.desc: Despite proximity to the river, fields in interior blocks like Thiruppanandal frequently fail to receive irrigation due to clogged canal endings.

problem.title: Bus Service Gaps
problem.desc: Public transport frequency to remote hamlets is nearly zero after 7 PM, stranding students and evening-shift workers.

problem.title: Brass Raw Material Cost
problem.desc: High prices of raw copper and zinc have made traditional vessel-making unprofitable for small artisans without government subsidies.

problem.title: School Infrastructure
problem.desc: Several rural government schools are operating in buildings that were declared unsafe years ago but have not been renovated.

consit.about: Known as the 'Temple Town,' Kumbakonam is the second largest city in the district and a vital commercial link between the delta and coastal regions. It is situated between the Cauvery and Arasalar rivers and serves as a major educational center.

problem.title: District Status Demand
problem.desc: The decade-long demand to make Kumbakonam a separate district headquarters remains unfulfilled, causing administrative delays for locals.

problem.title: Sacred Tank Pollution
problem.desc: Untreated sewage continues to leak into the Mahamaham tank, despite multiple Smart City cleaning initiatives in early 2026.

problem.title: Bypass Road Stalemate
problem.desc: The incomplete outer bypass road causes massive traffic jams inside the city during weekends and auspicious temple days.

problem.title: Hospital Overcrowding
problem.desc: The Headquarters Hospital is severely understaffed and lacks enough beds to handle the high volume of patients from surrounding taluks.

problem.title: Garbage Management
problem.desc: Lack of a modern waste processing plant has led to garbage being dumped in open canals, leading to health hazards in urban wards.

consit.about: An agrarian constituency established in 1951, Papanasam is a core part of the fertile rice bowl of Tamil Nadu. It is a major transit corridor on the Thanjavur-Kumbakonam highway, heavily reliant on paddy and sugarcane.

problem.title: Sugar Mill Payments
problem.desc: Sugarcane farmers are protesting against the local mill for failing to clear arrears totaling several crores for the last harvest cycle.

problem.title: Island Bridge Delay
problem.desc: Residents of Melaramanallur remain isolated without a permanent bridge across the Kollidam, relying on unsafe boats during monsoon floods.

problem.title: Hard Water Crisis
problem.desc: High TDS levels in groundwater have led to a surge in kidney-related ailments among the rural poor who cannot afford RO filters.

problem.title: Paddy Drying Hazards
problem.desc: Lack of community drying yards forces farmers to dry their harvest on the main highway, causing frequent fatal road accidents.

problem.title: Veterinary Doctor Vacancy
problem.desc: Rural veterinary centers are mostly closed or unstaffed, leaving cattle farmers without help during recent foot-and-mouth disease outbreaks.

consit.about: Famous for the Saint Thyagaraja Aradhana music festival, this constituency is the cultural capital of the delta. It is situated on the banks of five rivers and is a core zone for heritage tourism and riverbank agriculture.

problem.title: Banana Cold Storage
problem.desc: Despite being a top banana producer, the absence of cold storage forces farmers to sell to middlemen at 50% below market value.

problem.title: Cauvery Sewerage Entry
problem.desc: Untreated town sewage is directly released into the Cauvery, contaminating the water source for dozens of downstream villages.

problem.title: Canal Desilting Fraud
problem.desc: Local farmers allege that canal desilting is only done on paper, while the actual channels remain choked with silt and weeds.

problem.title: Traffic in Town Center
problem.desc: The lack of a proper bus terminal expansion in the town center creates permanent gridlock for inter-district mofussil buses.

problem.title: Wild Boar Menace
problem.desc: Farmers on the riverbanks are losing entire crops to wild boar attacks, with no effective prevention or compensation from the forest department.

consit.about: The district headquarters and a UNESCO World Heritage site featuring the Brihadeeswarar Temple. As a designated Smart City, it is the administrative heart and a major tourist destination for Chola-era heritage.

problem.title: Goods Shed Congestion
problem.desc: The location of the railway goods shed in the city center causes heavy lorry traffic that blocks Mary’s Corner for hours daily.

problem.title: Moat Reclamation Delay
problem.desc: The Smart City project to clean the historic moat has stalled, turning the waterbody into a stagnant breeding ground for mosquitoes.

problem.title: Youth Out-Migration
problem.desc: The total absence of IT parks or large manufacturing units has forced thousands of local graduates to leave the district for work.

problem.title: Open Drain Menace
problem.desc: Several inner-city lanes still have open sewage drains, posing a major sanitation risk to residents and visitors alike.

problem.title: Drinking Water Rationing
problem.desc: Uneven supply in newly added wards means some residents get water only once every four days during the summer months.

consit.about: Established in 1967, Orathanadu is a predominantly rural constituency known for its strong agricultural roots and cattle rearing. It serves as a vital link between the Thanjavur delta and the southern dry belts.

problem.title: Hydrocarbon Survey Fear
problem.desc: Residents are in a state of panic over rumors of secretive soil testing for hydrocarbon extraction, which they believe will ruin the soil.

problem.title: DPC Bribery Issues
problem.desc: Farmers at Direct Purchase Centres (DPCs) are being forced to pay handling charges of ₹40 per bag just to sell their paddy.

problem.title: Groundwater Salinity
problem.desc: The water table has dropped significantly, and the available water is turning salty, making it unfit for drinking and farming.

problem.title: College Transport
problem.desc: Rural female students face severe hardship due to the lack of buses during college hours, often forced to travel on foot-boards.

problem.title: Cattle Disease Compensation
problem.desc: Farmers whose cattle died in the recent Lumpy Skin disease outbreak have yet to receive the promised government compensation.

consit.about: A major commercial town and the 'Coconut Capital' of the district, situated on a dry plateau. It is a key trading center for the southern delta and the nearby coastal fishing villages.

problem.title: Coconut Pest Outbreak
problem.desc: The Whitefly pest has destroyed 40% of the coconut yield in 2026, yet no subsidy or scientific aid has reached the farmers.

problem.title: Incomplete Bypass
problem.desc: The Pattukkottai-Aranthangi bypass remains unfinished, forcing heavy trucks through the narrow main market streets.

problem.title: Copra Procurement Price
problem.desc: With no government procurement center in the town, farmers are selling copra to private agents at ₹30 below the Minimum Support Price.

problem.title: Saltwater Intrusion
problem.desc: Seawater is seeping into freshwater wells in the coastal border, killing coconut trees that were over 50 years old.

problem.title: Rail Frequency
problem.desc: Despite gauge conversion, the lack of a direct daily express train to Chennai is hampering the local coconut and coir trade.

consit.about: Created in 1967, this constituency is the southernmost part of the district and is entirely dependent on the Grand Anicut (GA) canal. It is a hub for coconut farming and small-scale coir manufacturing units.

problem.title: Tail-end Canal Silt
problem.desc: The GA canal is so silted in this block that water rarely reaches the last 15 villages, leading to permanent crop failure.

problem.title: No Govt Polytechnic
problem.desc: There is not a single government vocational institute in the taluk, leaving poor youth without skills for modern employment.

problem.title: Hospital Referral Crisis
problem.desc: The local GH is essentially a first-aid station; any major accident or emergency is sent 75km away to Thanjavur GH.

problem.title: Railway Underpass Flooding
problem.desc: Poorly designed railway underpasses flood during every rain, cutting off dozens of villages from the main market.

problem.title: Coir Industry Debt
problem.desc: Rising electricity costs and a drop in export demand have pushed dozens of local coir fiber units into bankruptcy.
